welcome_title: Welcome to the Probabilistic Reversal Learning Task
welcome_p1: This test measures your cognitive flexibility and reward-based learning. You will learn through trial and error which of two options is more likely to give you a reward.
welcome_p2: The twist: the rewarding option will switch during the test without warning. You need to detect the switch and adapt your choices. Feedback is probabilistic — even the better option does not always reward you.
tutorial_step1_text: You will see two options (A and B) side by side. One option has a higher chance of giving a reward than the other.
tutorial_step2_text: Click on the option you think is the better one. You will then see whether you received a reward (+) or not (−).
tutorial_step3_text: Use the feedback to figure out which option is currently better. Remember: the better option only rewards you most of the time, not every time.
tutorial_step4_text: At some point, the better option will switch. If you notice that your previously good option is no longer working, try the other one.
demo_step1: Two options are shown. One has a higher reward probability than the other. Which one is it?
demo_step2: Option A was chosen and a reward was received (+). This suggests A might be the better option.
demo_step3: Option B was chosen but no reward was received (−). This suggests B might not be the better option.
demo_step4: Option A was chosen again and rewarded. Consistent rewards confirm A is the better choice.
demo_step5: The rules have switched! Option A no longer gives a reward. Time to try the other option.
demo_step6: After the switch, option B now gives rewards. You adapted successfully!
demo_step7: You understand how it works. Ready to practice?
practice_complete_text1: Well done! You have a feel for how the task works.
practice_complete_text2: In the real test, try to maximize your total rewards by choosing the better option and adapting when the rules switch.
countdown_instruction: Click on the option you think is better
bottom_instruction: Click the option you think is more likely to give a reward
settings_total_trials_desc: Maximum number of trials per session
settings_high_prob_desc: Reward probability for the better option
settings_low_prob_desc: Reward probability for the worse option
settings_reversal_criterion_desc: Number of correct choices in the look-back window needed to trigger a reversal
settings_reversal_window_desc: Number of recent trials evaluated for the reversal criterion
settings_response_window_desc: Maximum time allowed to make a choice
settings_feedback_duration_desc: How long reward/no-reward feedback is shown
footer_description: The PRLT measures cognitive flexibility and reward-based decision making.
